fear: Being blamed for the leak simply because he is new and unknown — he knows how easily suspicion falls on the lowest man.
name: Elias Croft
role: Dock laborer and recent recruit to the Sons of Liberty
voice: Fast, earnest, slightly breathless — he over-explains and circles back to repeat himself when nervous; his loyalty is genuine but his manner invites suspicion.
secrets: He cannot read or write, which means he could not have authored the coded letter — a fact that exonerates him if the player thinks to ask.
He stole a small coin from the collection at Old South Meeting House last month and is terrified that sin will be used against him.
knowledge: He overheard Thomas Dillworth arguing in hushed tones with a man in a dark coat near Rowe's Wharf two nights ago — the stranger handed Dillworth something small.
He knows which crates on the Dartmouth are rigged to open quickly — he helped load them — and the information was never written down, only spoken aloud.
publicFace: A rough-handed, nervous young man who speaks too quickly and laughs at the wrong moments — he looks guilty even when he isn't.
represents: REQUIRES FACT-CHECK — verify whether this is a documented individual from the Underground Railroad network
trustLogic: He opens up immediately to anyone who treats him with basic respect and does not lead with accusations; he shuts down completely if he feels he is being set up as a scapegoat.
introAnchor: a rough-handed dock laborer and recent Sons of Liberty recruit who looks guilty even when he isn't
privateGoal: Prove himself to the Sons of Liberty and earn enough trust to be included in the boarding party tonight.

aggressionProfile.fleeStyle: Runs toward the waterfront and tries to hide among the crowd gathering at Griffin's Wharf.
aggressionProfile.chaseStyle: Fast on his feet and knows every alley near the docks; will hide rather than fight.
aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Stumbles over his words and repeats his alibi: 'I was on the dock all evening, ask any man there, I never left, not once.'
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will never reveal the stolen coin — he believes it is irrelevant but fears it will be used to destroy his character.
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: If formally accused in front of Samuel Adams without the player having any real evidence against him.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Becomes tearful and angry simultaneously: 'You want a name to give Adams, is that it? Well I won't give you mine for something I didn't do!'
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Breaks down and tells everything he knows about Dillworth and the stranger at Rowe's Wharf.
